name: Install and Configure Helm Charts
description: Install applications from Helm chart repositories, customize values, and verify successful deployments in your Kubernetes cluster.
trendingReason: Helm remains the de facto package manager for Kubernetes deployments

steps.title: Add the chart repository
steps.description: Register a Helm chart repository so you can search and install its charts.
steps.command: helm repo add bitnami https://charts.bitnami.com/bitnami && helm repo update
steps.tip: Always run 'helm repo update' after adding a repo to fetch the latest chart index.

steps.title: Search for available charts
steps.description: Browse the repository to find the chart you want to install.

steps.title: Install a chart with default values
steps.description: Deploy a chart into your cluster with a named release.
steps.tip: Use --create-namespace to automatically create the target namespace if it does not exist.

steps.title: Verify the deployment
steps.description: Check that the release was deployed successfully and pods are running.

steps.title: List all installed releases
steps.description: View all Helm releases across namespaces.

fullScript:
#!/bin/bash
# Helm Chart Install Workflow
set -euo pipefail

REPO_NAME=${1:?"Usage: $0 <repo-name> <repo-url> <chart> <release-name> <namespace>"}
REPO_URL=${2:?"Missing repo URL"}
CHART=${3:?"Missing chart name"}
RELEASE=${4:?"Missing release name"}
NAMESPACE=${5:-"default"}

echo "=== Adding Helm Repository ==="
helm repo add "$REPO_NAME" "$REPO_URL"
helm repo update

echo ""
echo "=== Searching for Chart ==="
helm search repo "$REPO_NAME/$CHART" --versions | head -5

echo ""
echo "=== Installing Chart ==="
helm install "$RELEASE" "$REPO_NAME/$CHART" \
  --namespace "$NAMESPACE" \
  --create-namespace \
  --wait --timeout 5m

echo ""
echo "=== Release Status ==="
helm status "$RELEASE" --namespace "$NAMESPACE"

echo ""
echo "=== Running Pods ==="
kubectl get pods -n "$NAMESPACE" -l "app.kubernetes.io/instance=$RELEASE"

faq.question: What is the difference between helm install and helm upgrade --install?
faq.answer: helm install only creates a new release and fails if one already exists. helm upgrade --install creates the release if it does not exist or upgrades it if it does, making it ideal for CI/CD pipelines.

faq.question: How do I install a specific version of a chart?
faq.answer: Use the --version flag: 'helm install my-release bitnami/nginx --version 15.0.0'. You can find available versions with 'helm search repo bitnami/nginx --versions'.

faq.question: Can I install a chart without connecting to a cluster?
faq.answer: You can use 'helm template' to render the manifests locally without a cluster connection. Use 'helm install --dry-run --debug' to simulate an install against the cluster API.
